title: Email Campaign Performance and Engagement
dataSources:
Unified envelope in `index=business` with vendor-normalized sourcetypes after Heavy Forwarder parsing — each bullet lists index, sourcetype, and ingestion mechanism.
• Salesforce Marketing Cloud Engagement REST — OAuth2 server-to-server — poll POST `https://{subdomain}.rest.marketingcloudapis.com/data/v1/customobjectdata/key/{{dataExtensionKey}}/rows` for `_Sent`, `_Open`, `_Click`, `_Bounce`, `_Unsubscribe` shaped staging writes mirrored nightly into Splunk via scripted pulls or warehouse unload → Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686) → `sourcetype="sfmc:email_events"`. Journey Builder extracts land through the Splunk Add-on for Salesforce bundle ([Splunkbase 3549](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3549)) when org policy exposes Engagement tracking extensions alongside Sales Cloud sObjects.
• Adobe Marketo Engage — REST `POST https://{munchkin}.mktorest.com/rest/v1/activities.json` with `activityTypeIds=10,11,6,7,8,12` (open=10, click=11, soft-bounce=6, hard-bounce=7, unsubscribe=8, spam-complaint=12); Bulk Extract CSV hourly → JDBC warehouse poll → DB Connect emit `sourcetype="marketo:activities"`.
• HubSpot Marketing Hub — REST `GET https://api.hubapi.com/email/public/v1/events` (`eventType` OPEN|CLICK|BOUNCE|UNSUBSCRIPTION|STATUSCHANGE); OAuth Private App tokens → modular poll → `sourcetype="hubspot:email_events"`.
• Mailchimp Marketing API — REST `GET https://{{dc}}.api.mailchimp.com/3.0/reports/{campaign_id}/email-activity` plus `GET .../reports/{campaign_id}/sent-to`; API key stored in Splunk credential locker → warehouse mart → DB Connect → `sourcetype="mailchimp:campaign_activity"`.
• Twilio SendGrid Event Webhook — HTTPS POST JSON arrays (`event_type` ∈ processed, deferred, delivered, open, click, bounce, dropped, spamreport, unsubscribe, group_unsubscribe, group_resubscribe) → Splunk HTTP Event Collector → `sourcetype="sendgrid:events"` with optional ECDSA signed-event verification.
• Salesforce Account Engagement (Pardot) — REST `GET https://pi.pardot.com/api/email/version/5/` query-style payloads ingested via TA REST modular input → `sourcetype="pardot:email_v5"`.
Warehouse-first stacks consolidate ESP extracts into Snowflake/BigQuery (`mart.email_engagement_facts`) polled through Splunk DB Connect with identical normalized columns regardless of upstream ESP.
app: Splunk Add-on for Salesforce ([Splunkbase 3549](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3549)) — Engagement-aligned REST extracts alongside Sales Cloud sObjects where packaged inputs exist; Splunk DB Connect ([Splunkbase 2686](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)) for JDBC-scheduled pulls from warehouse landing zones carrying Marketo, HubSpot, and Mailchimp columns; Splunk HTTP Event Collector (platform core — no Splunkbase identifier) terminating SendGrid Event Webhooks on the Heavy Forwarder or ingest tier; optional Splunk IT Service Intelligence KPI bindings when promoting digest metrics into glass tables.
spl:
index=business (sourcetype="sfmc:email_events" OR sourcetype="marketo:activities" OR sourcetype="hubspot:email_events" OR sourcetype="mailchimp:campaign_activity" OR sourcetype="sendgrid:events" OR sourcetype="pardot:email_v5") earliest=-30d
| eval campaign_key=lower(trim(coalesce(campaign_id, JobID, campaignId, sg_campaign_id, email_campaign_Id)))
| eval subscriber_key=coalesce(subscriber_key, RecipientID, leadId, recipient_email_hash, md5(lower(trim(coalesce(email,to_string(recipient))))))
| eval send_id=coalesce(email_send_id, JobID, campaign_message_id, sg_message_id, batch_id)
| eval evt_raw=lower(coalesce(event_type, activityTypeName, sg_event_type, action, primaryAttributeValue))
| eval delivered_evt=if(match(evt_raw,"delivered|sent|processed|delivery"),1,0)
| eval open_evt=if(match(evt_raw,"^(open|opened)$"),1,0)
| eval click_evt=if(match(evt_raw,"^(click|clicked)$"),1,0)
| eval bounce_evt=if(match(evt_raw,"bounce|blocked|dropped"),1,0)
| eval spam_evt=if(match(evt_raw,"spam|spamreport|spam_complaint"),1,0)
| eval unsub_evt=if(match(evt_raw,"unsubscribe|group_unsubscribe|opt.?out"),1,0)
| eval send_epoch=tonumber(coalesce(mail_send_epoch, sent_epoch_ms))
| eval evt_epoch=_time
| eval lag_hours=if(isnotnull(send_epoch) AND send_epoch<=evt_epoch AND (open_evt=1 OR click_evt=1 OR unsub_evt=1 OR spam_evt=1), round((evt_epoch-send_epoch)/3600,5), null())
| eval velocity_bucket=case(isnull(lag_hours),"unknown", lag_hours<1,"immediate", lag_hours<=24,"delayed", lag_hours<=168,"long_tail",1=1,"extended")
| stats sum(delivered_evt) as delivered_ct sum(open_evt) as opens sum(click_evt) as clicks sum(bounce_evt) as bounces sum(spam_evt) as spam_reports sum(unsub_evt) as unsubs dc(subscriber_key) as uniq_rcpt by campaign_key velocity_bucket send_id
| eventstats sum(delivered_ct) as cohort_delivered sum(opens) as tot_opens sum(clicks) as tot_clicks
| eval cohort_engagements=tot_opens+tot_clicks
| eval engagement_rate_pct=if(delivered_ct>0, round(100*(opens+clicks)/delivered_ct,3), null())
| eval deliverability_rate_pct=if((delivered_ct+bounces)>0, round(100*delivered_ct/(delivered_ct+bounces),3), null())
| eval bounce_share=if(delivered_ct>0, round(bounces/nullif(delivered_ct,0),5), null())
| eval spam_share=if(delivered_ct>0, round(spam_reports/nullif(delivered_ct,0),5), null())
| eval deliverability_alarm=if(deliverability_rate_pct<97 OR bounce_share>0.02 OR spam_share>0.001 OR cohort_engagements/nullif(cohort_delivered,0)<0.015,1,0)
| sort - deliverability_alarm engagement_rate_pct
| table campaign_key velocity_bucket send_id delivered_ct opens clicks bounces spam_reports unsubs uniq_rcpt engagement_rate_pct deliverability_rate_pct bounce_share spam_share deliverability_alarm cohort_delivered cohort_engagements
description: This detection stitches ESP-neutral Splunk rows across Salesforce Marketing Cloud Journey sends, Marketo activity pulls, HubSpot mail events, Mailchimp campaign summaries, SendGrid webhook bursts, and Pardot v5 queries — normalizing identifiers with coalesce() across campaign_key, send_id, subscriber_key, and salted recipient hashes — then computes opens, clicks, soft and hard bounces, spam complaints, and unsubscribes per campaign per blast. It layers engagement-velocity buckets (immediate under one hour versus delayed one-to-twenty-four hours versus long-tail one-to-seven days) using lag_hours between authoritative mail_send_epoch and reaction timestamps so analysts distinguish fatigue-driven decay from ESP propagation skew. Splunk raises deliverability-rate alarms when inbox placement erosion or authentication drift pushes bounce_share above two percent or spam_share above ten basis points versus Litmus benchmark rails.
value: Industry benchmarks position broadcast outreach among the strongest revenue levers — Litmus State of Email 2024 cites roughly thirty-six dollars back per dollar invested — meaning modest dips in inbox placement translate straight into measurable pipeline misses before CRM-attributed dashboards react. Monitoring normalized engagement alongside bounce and complaint telemetry protects sender reputation tracked externally via Microsoft SNDS, Google Postmaster Tools, Sender Score, or Talos listings because mailbox providers penalize IPs or domains silently until suppression spikes surface internally. Aligning Splunk alerts with CAN-SPAM ten-day suppression obligations, CASL consent lineage, GDPR Article Seven withdrawal artifacts, PECR newsletter standards, CCPA opt-out honoring, LGPD bases, and POPIA processing narratives turns operational dashboards into evidentiary guardrails rather than vanity charts.
implementation: Land REST extracts or warehouse snapshots into business indexes with stable sourcetypes per ESP; normalize timestamps so mail_send_epoch reflects ESP-sent time rather than indexer receipt time; terminate SendGrid POST payloads on HTTP Event Collector with TLS offloading on Heavy Forwarders; save the SPL as rpt_email_campaign_engagement_velocity and attach Dashboard Studio tiles plus threshold alerts tied to bounce_share and spam_share ceilings documented jointly with Legal.
visualization: Dashboard Studio layout: splunk.line chart of engagement_rate_pct over calendar days split by velocity_bucket for cohort storytelling; splunk.gauge comparing deliverability_rate_pct versus a ninety-seven percent guardrail target per ESP vendor token; splunk.Sankey (or splunk.parallelcoordinates) illustrating subscriber lifecycle hops Sent → Delivered → Immediate engagement → Delayed engagement → Suppressed; splunk.heatmap binning mail_send_hour versus mail_send_wday with median lag_hours coloring cells for send-time experimentation; splunk.table ranking campaign_key rows by clicks while exposing bounce_share columns for remediation triage.

references.title: Salesforce — Marketing Cloud Engagement REST API (custom object data)
Adobe Marketo Engage REST — Activities endpoint
Splunk — Scale HTTP Event Collector throughput

knownFalsePositives: Privacy-enhanced mailbox vendors prefetch invisible pixels minutes post-send — Splunk logs mimic curiosity bursts absent incremental link taps unless analysts overlay click-centric companion tiles beside headline charts. TLS handshake retries inside Asian transit carriers occasionally duplicate webhook deliveries seconds apart — transient bounce_evt duplication resembles harsh reputation swings until dedupe macros keyed on sg_message_id collapse repeats. Bulk seasonal sends aligned to retail peaks inflate baseline comparisons against SaaS-heavy Splunk cohorts — dashboards resemble ESP outages although cohort mixes shifted toward bargain hunters opening everything yet clicking nothing substantive. Warehouse CDC lag replaying ninety-day unsubscribe horizons backward collapses unsubscribe velocity histograms tied to legal holds unrelated to creative merit — Splunk timelines resemble orchestrated churn rather than dated audience fatigue unless immutable snapshots isolate immutable weekly extracts before Legal revises CRM consent artifacts retroactively.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — KPI promotion from saved searches into glass tables

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud roles covering `index=business` searches, HTTP Event Collector token administration, and Splunk DB Connect JDBC credential objects with least-privilege warehouse accounts scoped to `mart.email_engagement_facts` (or equivalent) read-only grants.
• Network egress from Heavy Forwarders to Salesforce Marketing Cloud REST hosts (`*.marketingcloudapis.com`), Adobe Marketo hosts (`*.mktorest.com`), HubSpot `api.hubapi.com`, Mailchimp regional API hosts, SendGrid webhook ingress targets, and optional Pardot `pi.pardot.com` — align TLS inspection policies so intermediaries do not strip OAuth Authorization headers.
• Canonical field worksheet (`campaign_key`, `send_id`, `subscriber_key`, `mail_send_epoch`, `esp_name`, `tenant_id`) signed by Marketing Operations before Splunk cutover so macros stay stable across MAP migrations.

**Step 1 — Configure data collection**
Install Splunk Add-on for Salesforce (Splunkbase 3549) on collection nodes authorized for Marketing Cloud OAuth when Engagement REST pulls share credentials with Core — otherwise deploy dedicated Python modular inputs referencing `/data/v1/customobjectdata/key/{{dataExtensionKey}}/rows` with client_id + client_secret in Splunk secure storage (**Settings → Passwords**). Encode stack constants (`S1`, `S10`, tenant subdomain) inside macros such as `sfmc_rest_base`. Schedule nightly hydration of `_Sent` grains before `_Open` joins because velocity math treats mail_send_epoch as authoritative origin time mirrored into epoch milliseconds.

Marketo Engage: configure REST paging against `/rest/v1/activities.json` — persist nextPageToken checkpoints inside KV Store so Splunk restarts do not replay duplicate opens during pagination storms. Mirror batches into Snowflake via native CDC when Splunk DB Connect JDBC pulls `mart.marketo_activity_rows` hourly (`cron 15 * * * *`) emitting `sourcetype="marketo:activities"` with lowercase mirrors (`leadid`, `activitytypeid`, `activitydate`).

HubSpot Marketing Hub: authenticate Private Apps (**Settings → Integrations → Private Apps**) — scopes covering marketing-email read categories — query `/email/public/v1/events` with sliding `_since` windows anchored on KV watermark `hubspot_email_watermark`. Respect HubSpot burst tiers (~100 calls per ten seconds).

Mailchimp: orchestrate `/3.0/reports/{campaign_id}/email-activity` via your scheduler — DB Connect reads aggregated mart rows rather than hammering Mailchimp interactively from Splunk Cloud workers lacking static egress.

SendGrid/Twilio: terminate Event Webhook POST arrays on dedicated HEC tokens — preserve nested reason codes inside bounce payloads instead of flattening prematurely so spam_evt classification survives governance reviews.

Pardot v5: normalize `/api/email/version/5/` responses through props/transforms before `_time` assignment — vintage tenants emit XML-shaped bodies requiring consistent JSON coercion.

**Step 2 — Encode normalization macros and saved searches**
Author macros.conf entries such as `esp_normalize_evt(evt_raw)` collapsing vendor synonyms (`Opened`, `Email Opened`, `Clicked Email`) into canonical buckets deliver/open/click/bounce/spam/unsubscribe.

Maintain lookup `esp_campaign_rollups.csv` keyed by esp_name plus legacy nurture slugs — shields YoY dashboards after Journey rename weekends remap Salesforce Job IDs.

Clone SPL into saved searches `rpt_email_campaign_eng_velocity` plus `alert_bad_deliverability` filtering `deliverability_alarm==1` — throttle twelve hours per campaign_key to absorb intentional sunset batches.

Optional Splunk ITSI KPI base searches aggregate throughput feeding glass tiles — align KPI thresholds with Splunk Enterprise alert macros so operational and premium tiers disagree rarely during audits.

**Step 3 — Validate against ESP consoles**
Sample five random send_id values — reconcile Splunk delivered_ct sums against Salesforce Marketing Cloud Email Studio Sent totals within ±two percent cadence tolerance bounded by ingestion latency.

Cross-read HubSpot Email Performance tabs — OPEN versus CLICK totals trend together though behavioral clocks differ ±fifteen minutes from Splunk `_time`; document tolerance inside runbook footnotes.

SendGrid: compare webhook counts against Twilio Activity diagnostics — reconcile dropped versus deferred labels against SendGrid bounce dictionaries (`blocked`, `invalid`, `spam`, `technical`) before executives trust Splunk ratios.

Mailchimp: export CSV from UI for identical campaign_id — Splunk cohort sizes should mirror unique email_id tallies modulo post-export suppression churn.

**Step 4 — Operationalize dashboards and alerting**
Publish Dashboard Studio workspace `email_ops_velocity` binding visualization tokens from the `visualization` field — default picker last thirty days — trellis panels by esp_name so hybrid stacks visualize side-by-side without duplicated workbooks.

Route `deliverability_alarm==1` to Deliverability Guild covering Marketing Ops, Messaging Infrastructure, Brand Security — suppress during maintenance-window lookups tied to planned SendGrid credential rotations.

Embed data-quality tiles counting unknown velocity_bucket share — halt executive digests automatically when missing mail_send_epoch exceeds six percent of rows until parent send lookups rerun successfully.

**Step 5 — Troubleshooting**
• Apple Mail Privacy Protection prefetch inflates measured opens thirty-to-fifty percent across iOS-heavy cohorts — pivot executive tiles toward click-centric composites whenever privacy-enhanced mailbox share crosses governance rails fed by User-Agent enrichment sidecars.
• Gmail Promotions tab placement suppresses reactions without SMTP bounce telemetry — weekly joins against Google Postmaster Tools exports contextualize depressed Splunk reactions absent infrastructure regressions.
• Microsoft 365 Defender Safe Links rewriting yields clustered clicks milliseconds apart inside tenant egress IPs — augment SPL with ASN dictionaries tagging gateway fingerprints before CTR thresholds declare creative victories falsely.
• Outlook desktop blocking remote images suppresses beacon fires entirely — velocity buckets skew toward delayed-only engagement despite inbox curiosity — instruct stakeholders to weigh clicks plus textual replies mirrored via CRM ingest lanes rather than headline open ratios alone.
• ESP hyperlink rewriting strips marketers’ trailing UTM pairs — reconcile enriched classifications inside Adobe Analytics or GA4 exports rather than flawed Splunk-only merges against rewritten URLs.
• Scripted crawler farms rotating commodity subnets inflate CTR against outbound nurture cadences — enrich Splunk rows with threat-intel ranges and tighten geo fences before attributing influenced pipeline dollars from scripted noise bursts.
• Subdomain DKIM selectors divergent from SPF-aligned envelope domains intermittently categorize transient TLS faults as fatal hard-bounces — coordinate DNS TTL reductions ahead of migrations isolating bounce_evt spikes unrelated to creative edits.
• SendGrid asynchronous webhook tuples occasionally arrive spamreport ahead of processed — reorder streams using transaction keyed on stable sg_message_id spanning sixty-second windows before velocity histogram commits.
• CAN-SPAM mandated ten-business-day suppression grace legally permits stray touches briefly post-capture — annotate dashboards whenever Legal distinguishes lawful continuation touches versus accidental duplicates landing outside documented windows.
• GDPR withdrawal timestamps retroactively altering consent lineage distort longitudinal unsubscribe cohort charts — isolate immutable Splunk summary snapshots capturing weekly baselines before Legal revises Salesforce consent rows backward mid-quarter.
• Global audiences blasted using UTC anchors skew velocity histograms versus PST-heavy bases — normalize experiments using recipient-local send-hour macros sourced from demographic tables before judging creative lift fairly.
• Rendering stacks (Outlook MSO versus Apple WebKit versus Gmail CSS pipelines) diverge tracked link structures — hover-sensitive CSS yields divergent clicks across clients — split mobile versus desktop cohorts inside heatmaps before blaming copywriters for instrumentation seams.
• Suppression propagation latency between Salesforce Marketing Cloud, Marketo, and HubSpot yields duplicate deliveries crossing CAN-SPAM optics despite Splunk-perfect ingestion — institute nightly reconciliation sourcetype `suppression_audit` bridging ESP mismatch investigations across stacks.

Additional Step 1 depth — volume planning and transforms
Expect webhook-heavy seasons (Black Friday, fiscal close mailstorms) to spike SendGrid throughput beyond baseline DB Connect pulls — assign dedicated Heavy Forwarder listener pools with separate connection_host queues so burst batches do not contend with hourly JDBC snapshots. Configure props.conf FIELDALIAS pairs mapping vendor-native identifiers (`job_id`, `campaign_activity_id`, `mc_campaign_id`) onto canonical campaign_key before macros execute — prevents brittle SPL edits whenever MAP admins rename Journey folders yet preserve opaque IDs underneath.

Additional Step 4 depth — stakeholder routing and cadence
Publish annotated Dashboard Studio notes tying deliverability_alarm spikes to contemporaneous DNS changes logged inside Change tickets — inbox placement dips frequently correlate with BIMI vet rotations or DMARC policy tightenings unrelated to Splunk ingestion fidelity.
grandmaExplanation: We follow ordinary newsletters people asked for—whether anyone bothers tapping buttons, quietly walks away from lists, or warns providers mail feels shady—so storytellers freshen wording before noisy gripes pile up.
